html:
[4647]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 葬父母費用億計 - Funeral expenses in the tens of millions, emphasizing the enormous cost.
  • 哭聲盈衢 - Cries filling the streets, indicating widespread mourning or distress.
  • 大霖雨震曜父墓門屋 - Heavy rain shaking the door and roof of Duan's father's tomb, a dramatic description of the storm.
  • 大風飄發父寢堂於外垣五十余步 - A strong wind blowing the father's sleeping hall outside the wall by more than fifty steps, emphasizing the force of the wind.
  • 松柏植以成林 - Pine and cypress trees planted to form a forest, indicating a well-maintained tomb.
  • 至是悉枯死 - By then, all of them had withered and died, a metaphor for the curse or divine retribution.
  • 竟為石勒所擒 - Finally captured by Shi Le, indicating the ultimate downfall of Duan Yao.
  • 有回風從東來入裔船中 - A whirlwind coming from the east entering Duan Yi's boat, a supernatural event.
  • 狀如匹練長五六丈 - Shaped like a ribbon, about five or six zhang long, describing the appearance of the wind.
  • 有刀兵死喪之亂 - There will be a disaster of war and death, a prediction of doom.
  • 有大風從門而入直來沖車 - A strong wind coming from the door directly hitting the carriage, a dramatic description of the event.
  • 車葢覆向天 - The canopy of the carriage overturned towards the sky, indicating the severity of the wind.
  • 是年明帝立合門诛 - That year, Emperor Ming ascended the throne and all the officials were executed, a historical event.
  • 五色旛風吹飛入云中 - A five-colored flag blown by the wind into the clouds, a supernatural event.
  • 半日乃下 - After half a day, it came down, indicating the duration of the flag's flight.
  • 幡者事當翻覆 - The flag means that affairs will be overturned, a metaphor for political upheaval.
  • 數日而恵景敗 - Several days later, Cui Huijing was defeated, indicating the outcome of the event.
  • 轉為錄尚书 - Transferred to the position of Grand Secretary, indicating a change in office.
  • 拜命其夜暴风震雷 - On the night of receiving the appointment, there was a violent wind and thunder, a supernatural event.
  • 拔庭中桐树六十围者倒立本处 - A sixty-gu-wide paulownia tree in the courtyard was uprooted and stood upright in its original place, a dramatic description of the storm.
  • 識者知其不终 - Those who recognized it knew that it would not end well, indicating a prediction of doom.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 前赵刘曜 - Duan Yao, a ruler of the Former Zhao dynasty.
  • 东晋成帝 - Emperor Cheng of Eastern Jin, a ruler of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 浔阳 - Xunyang, a city in modern-day Jiangxi Province.
  • 北海王许世宗 - Xu Shizong, a prince of the Northern Qi dynasty.
  • 北齐 - Northern Qi, a dynasty that ruled northern China.

Proper Nouns

  • 劉曜 - Duan Yao, a ruler of the Former Zhao dynasty.
  • 東晉成帝 - Emperor Cheng of Eastern Jin, a ruler of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 劉裔 - Duan Yi, a person mentioned in the text.
  • 崔恵景 - Cui Huijing, a person mentioned in the text.
  • 許世宗 - Xu Shizong, a prince of the Northern Qi dynasty.
  • 北齊 - Northern Qi, a dynasty that ruled northern China.

[4648]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 初嫁夕車至西州而疾風大起 - On the night of her wedding, as the carriage arrived at Xizhou, a strong wind suddenly arose.
  • 發屋折木 - Winds that blew off roofs and snapped trees.
  • 雪霰交下 - Snow and sleet fell together.
  • 帷帘皆白 - The curtains and screens were all white.
  • 大雷电 - Thunder and lightning.
  • 兩柱俱碎 - Both pillars were shattered.
  • 不祥 - Omen of ill fortune.
  • 淫 - Lecherous behavior (uncertain reading, marked as '' indicates a questionable character).
  • 不终之应 - An omen of premature death.
  • 設壇刑白馬祭天 - Set up an altar, sacrifice a white horse, and worship heaven.
  • 稱魏公 - Claimed the title of Prince of Wei.
  • 置僚佐 - Appointed officials.
  • 改元升壇 - Changed the era name and ascended the altar.
  • 黑風從西北暴至 - A black wind suddenly arose from the northwest.
  • 吹密衣冠及左右僚屬皆倒於壇下 - The wind blew his clothes and those of his officials and attendants, causing them to fall backward on the altar.
  • 沙塵暗天 - Sand and dust obscured the sky.
  • 咫尺不相見 - Could not see each other even at arm's length.
  • 良久乃息 - After a long time, it subsided.
  • 賊軍惡之 - The rebel army disliked this.
  • 俄而密敗 - Soon after, Li Mi was defeated.
  • 斷虹飲於宮池 - A broken rainbow descended into the palace pond.
  • 漸漸縮小化為男子 - Gradually shrank and transformed into a man.
  • 着黃赤紫之間衣 - Wearing clothes of yellow, red, and purple.
  • 入樹良久不出 - Entered the tree and did not emerge for a long time.
  • 世隆怪異 - Xia Shilong found this strange.
  • 見男子為大赤蛇盤繞 - Saw the man as a large red snake coiled.
  • 眾懼不敢逼 - Everyone was afraid and dared not approach.
  • 而少年遙擲瓦礫 - But the young men threw tiles and stones from a distance.
  • 聞樹中有聲極異如婦人之哭 - Heard a very strange sound from inside the tree, like a woman's weeping.
  • 須臾雲霧不相見 - After a moment, clouds and mist obscured the view.
  • 又聞如遠雷之響 - Again heard a rumbling sound like distant thunder.
  • 俄有一彩龍與赤鵠飛去 - Soon, a colorful dragon and a red crane flew away.
  • 及曉世隆往觀之 - When dawn came, Xia Shilong went to look at it.
  • 見中紫蛇皮及五色蛟皮 - Saw purple snake skin and five-colored dragon skin in the tree.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁元帝妃徐妃 - Empress Xu of Emperor Yuan of Liang.
  • 梁元帝 - Emperor Yuan of Liang, a ruler of the Southern Dynasties.
  • 李密 - Li Mi, a rebel leader during the Sui Dynasty.
  • 隋大業十三年 - The thirteenth year of the Daye era of the Sui Dynasty (617 AD).
  • 鞏縣 - Gong County, a place in Henan Province.
  • 魏公 - Prince of Wei, a title claimed by Li Mi.
  • 黑風 - Black wind, often associated with ominous signs in Chinese folklore.
  • 東甌後記 - Eastern Ou Postscript, a historical record from the Eastern Ou region.

Proper Nouns

  • 徐妃 - Empress Xu.
  • 梁元帝 - Emperor Yuan of Liang.
  • 李密 - Li Mi.
  • 隋大業十三年 - The thirteenth year of the Daye era of the Sui Dynasty.
  • 鞏縣 - Gong County.
  • 魏公 - Prince of Wei.
  • 東甌後記 - Eastern Ou Postscript.

[4649]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 為州吏 - Serving as a state official, where '州' refers to a state or province, and '吏' refers to a minor official.
  • 其婦秦 - His wife Qin, where '秦' is the surname of the wife.
  • 色耀 - With a dazzling color, where '' (xuàn) means 'dazzling' or 'bright'.
  • 相感接 - To interact or engage with each other, where '感' (gǎn) means 'to feel' or 'to interact,' and '接' (jiē) means 'to connect' or 'to meet'.
  • 如是積年 - For many years, where '如是' (rú shì) means 'like this' or 'thus,' and '積年' (jī nián) means 'for many years'.
  • 村人觀其所至 - Villagers watched where they went, where '觀' (guān) means 'to watch' or 'to observe,' and '其所至' (qí suǒ zhì) means 'where they went'.
  • 辄有虹见 - There was always a rainbow seen, where '辄' (zhé) means 'always' or 'often,' and '虹' (hóng) means 'rainbow'.
  • 引水共饮 - To draw water and drink together, where '引' (yǐn) means 'to draw' or 'to lead,' and '共饮' (gòng yǐn) means 'to drink together'.
  • 遂有身 - She then became pregnant, where '遂' (suì) means 'then' or 'thus,' and '有身' (yǒu shēn) means 'to become pregnant'.
  • 生而如人多肉 - Born like a human with much flesh, where '如人' (rú rén) means 'like a human,' and '多肉' (duō ròu) means 'with much flesh'.
  • 内于盆中 - Placed in a basin, where '内' (nèi) means 'to place inside,' and '盆' (pén) means 'basin'.
  • 自衣 - Dressed herself, where '自' (zì) means 'by oneself,' and '衣' (yī) means 'to dress'.
  • 以绛嚢盛 - Stored in a red bag, where '以' (yǐ) means 'with' or 'using,' '绛' (jiàng) means 'crimson,' '嚢' (náng) means 'bag,' and '盛' (shèng) means 'to store' or 'to hold'.
  • 时出与乳 - Came out sometimes to nurse, where '时' (shí) means 'sometimes,' '出' (chū) means 'to come out,' '与' (yǔ) means 'together with,' and '乳' (rǔ) means 'to nurse' or 'to breastfeed'.
  • 风雨 - Rain and wind, where '风' (fēng) means 'wind,' and '雨' (yǔ) means 'rain'.
  • 省母 - Visited her mother, where '省' (shěng) means 'to visit' or 'to inspect,' and '母' (mǔ) means 'mother'.
  • 适田 - Went to the fields, where '适' (shì) means 'to go to,' and '田' (tián) means 'fields'.
  • 畏之 - Fear it, where '畏' (wèi) means 'to fear,' and '之' (zhī) means 'it'.
  • 须臾 - A moment or in a short while, where '须臾' (xū yú) is an idiomatic expression for a brief period of time.
  • 絶 - To cease or to stop, where '絶' (jué) means 'to cut off' or 'to stop'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庐陵巴邱 - Refers to a place in the ancient region of Lu Ling, which is now part of modern-day Jiangxi Province. '巴邱' (Bā Qiū) is a specific location within this region.
  • 神异 - Refers to a collection of stories about supernatural phenomena, where '神异' (shén yì) means 'supernatural' or 'miraculous,' and '' (zhuàn) means 'transmission' or 'record'.
  • 虹 - In this context, the rainbow is not just a natural phenomenon but is also seen as a sign of divine or supernatural activity, often associated with the presence of deities or spirits.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈济 - The name of the state official.
  • 秦 - The surname of Tien's wife.
  • 庐陵巴邱 - A specific location in ancient China.
  • 神异 - The title of the collection of supernatural stories.

[4650]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 虹飲其釜鬲噏響便竭 - Vivid description of a rainbow drinking from a pot or vessel, causing it to empty. '虹' (rainbow) is personified as drinking.
  • 辇酒灌之 - '辇' (辇) means to carry by a carriage or cart, here it implies bringing wine to pour into the pot.
  • 随投随竭 - As soon as wine is poured, the pot empties again.
  • 吐金满器 - The pot spews out gold, filling it up.
  • 日益隆富 - Becomes increasingly wealthy and prosperous.
  • 白虹入室就饮其粥 - A white rainbow enters the room and drinks the porridge.
  • 掷器于阶 - Throws the pot onto the steps.
  • 遂作风雨声振于庭户 - Causes a wind and rain sound, shaking the hall and doors.
  • 化为女子年如十六七 - Transforms into a girl around sixteen or seventeen years old.
  • 声如钟磬 - Makes a sound like bells and chimes.
  • 化为虹而上天 - Transforms back into a rainbow and ascends to heaven.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 东晋 - The Eastern Jin Dynasty (317-420 CE).
  • 义熙 - The era name of Emperor Shun of Jin.
  • 晋陵 - A region in present-day Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 虹 - In Chinese folklore, rainbows are often associated with good luck and prosperity.
  • 辇 - A type of carriage or cart used for carrying people or goods.
  • 宋 - The Southern Song Dynasty (420-479 CE).
  • 长沙王 - A title given to a nobleman in the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 广陵 - An ancient name for Yangzhou, a city in present-day Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 后魏 - The Northern Wei Dynasty (386-534 CE).
  • 正光 - The era name of Emperor Xiaoming of Northern Wei.
  • 首阳山 - A mountain in present-day Henan Province, China.
  • 蒲津 - A place name, likely referring to a military post or garrison.
  • 明帝 - The emperor of the Northern Wei Dynasty.
  • 天女 - A celestial maiden, a divine female figure in Chinese mythology.

Proper Nouns

  • 薛愿 - A historical figure.
  • 刘义庆 - A historical figure.
  • 宋长沙王道邻子义庆 - Refers to Liu Yiqing, the son of Dao Lin, the King of Changsha.
  • 后魏明帝 - Emperor Xiaoming of Northern Wei.
  • 首阳山 - A mountain in present-day Henan Province, China.
  • 蒲津 - A place name, likely referring to a military post or garrison.
  • 宇文显 - A historical figure, likely a military commander.

[4651]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 暴风雨俄顷而霁 - A vivid description of a sudden storm and its quick dissipation.
  • 虹蜺自空而下直入庭垂首于筵 - A poetic description of a rainbow descending into the hall and bending over the table.
  • 吸其食饮且尽 - Means 'drank and ate almost all.'
  • 首如驴霏然若晴霞状红碧相霭虚空五色四视左右久而方去 - A vivid and somewhat surreal description of the rainbow's appearance, suggesting it looked like a donkey's head with colors resembling a rainbow and a mix of red and blue, appearing in the air with multiple colors and looking around before leaving.
  • 公惧且恶之遂罢宴 - Means 'the governor was both afraid and disgusted, so he stopped the banquet.'
  • 真天下祥符也固不为人之怪耳 - Means 'it is truly a sign of good fortune in the world, and it is not a strange thing for humans.'
  • 夫虹蜺天使也降于邪则为戾降于正则为祥理宜然矣 - A philosophical statement about rainbows being messengers of heaven, which can bring misfortune if they descend to evil and good fortune if they descend to righteousness.
  • 公正人也是宜为庆为祥敢以前贺 - Means 'since you are an upright person, this should be a cause for celebration and good fortune, so I dare to congratulate you beforehand.'
  • 具以帛书其语而献 - Means 'he wrote the words on a silk cloth and presented them.'
  • 后旬余有诏就拜中书令 - Means 'a few weeks later, there was an imperial edict appointing him as the Chief Secretary.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 賔客从事十余人 - Refers to Wei Gao's guests and officials.
  • 郡西亭 - Refers to a pavilion in the western part of the city.
  • 虹蜺 - Refers to a rainbow, which in Chinese culture often has symbolic meanings.
  • 中书令 - A high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty, responsible for drafting imperial edicts.
  • 豆卢署 - Refers to Doulu Shu, a former official who was a guest in Sichuan.
  • 诏 - Refers to an imperial edict.

Proper Nouns

  • 豆卢署 - Doulu Shu, a former official.
  • 中书令 - A high-ranking official position.

[4652]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 瓮峰 - A mountain or peak, often used in geographical descriptions.
  • 夸父山 - Refers to a specific mountain, possibly named after the mythological figure Kuafu.
  • 插灶 - Literally 'insert stove,' likely a metaphor or description of a specific feature or activity.
  • 河山石斛 - Refers to a type of orchid or a specific plant found in mountainous regions.
  • 终南乳洞 - A cave in the Zhongnan Mountains, known for its unique features.
  • 古铁鏁 - An ancient iron lock, possibly a significant artifact or feature.
  • 崖山 - A cliff or mountain, often used in geographical descriptions.
  • 圣钟山 - A mountain with a sacred bell, likely a place of religious significance.
  • 嵩梁山 - A mountain, possibly named after the legendary figure Songliang.
  • 石鼓山 - A mountain with stone drums, likely a place of historical or cultural importance.
  • 射的山 - A mountain, possibly named after a target or shooting range.
  • 怪山 - A mountain with unusual or strange features.
  • 鸣铙山 - A mountain where bells or drums are heard, likely a place of natural or cultural significance.
  • 贑台 - A platform or stage, possibly a significant location.
  • 上霄峯 - A peak in the upper heavens, likely a poetic or metaphorical description.
  • 麦积山 - A mountain, possibly named after a specific feature or location.
  • 斗山观 - A mountain with a temple or observatory, likely a place of religious or cultural significance.
  • 大竹路 - A road or path through a bamboo forest, likely a scenic or significant route.
  • 溪 - A stream or small river, often used in geographical descriptions.
  • 溪毒 - A toxic stream or river, possibly a dangerous or unusual feature.
  • 玉笥山 - A mountain, possibly named after a specific feature or location.
  • 大翮山 - A mountain with large wings, possibly a metaphorical or descriptive name.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han, a significant ruler of the Han dynasty.
  • 玉笥山 - A mountain associated with Emperor Wu of Han and a sacred event.
  • 王次仲 - A historical figure who transformed ancient script into modern calligraphy.
  • 秦始皇 - Emperor Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of a unified China.
  • 仓颉 - The legendary creator of Chinese characters.
  • 隶书 - A type of Chinese calligraphy.
  • 大翮山 - A mountain with a story involving a transformation into a bird.
  • 温汤 - A hot spring, often associated with healing properties.

Proper Nouns

  • 汉武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 玉笥山 - A specific mountain.
  • 王次仲 - A historical figure.
  • 秦始皇 - Emperor Qin Shi Huang.
  • 仓颉 - The legendary creator of Chinese characters.
  • 隶书 - A type of Chinese calligraphy.
  • 大翮山 - A specific mountain.
  • 温汤 - A specific hot spring.

[4653]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吳天門張蓋冬月 - Proper name and title, likely referring to a specific person or official during the winter in Wu Tianmen.
  • 毛而不衣 - Literally means 'having hair but not wearing clothes,' describing a creature with hair but no clothing.
  • 負數頭死猿 - Carrying several dead monkeys, describes the creature carrying dead monkeys.
  • 與語不应 - When spoken to, it did not respond, indicates the creature's lack of response.
  • 因将归闭空屋中 - So they took it back and locked it in an empty room, describes the action of taking the creature to a room and locking it.
  • 十余日 - More than ten days, indicates a period of time.
  • 复送故处 - Returned it to its original place, describes the action of returning the creature.
  • 孫皓時 - During the reign of Sun Hao, refers to a specific historical period.
  • 臨海得毛人 - Found a hairy person in Linhai, describes the discovery of a hairy person in the region of Linhai.
  • 山海經云 - According to the *Shanhaijing* (Classic of Mountains and Seas), introduces a reference to an ancient text.
  • 山精如人面有毛 - Mountain spirits look like humans with hair, describes the appearance of mountain spirits.
  • 抱朴子曰 - Zhang Ping said in *Baopu Zi*, introduces a reference to another ancient text.
  • 山之精形如小儿而独足足向后 - The mountain spirit looks like a child with one foot pointing backward, describes the appearance of the mountain spirit.
  • 喜来犯人 - It likes to attack people, describes the behavior of the mountain spirit.
  • 其名蚑知而呼之即当自却 - Its name is Qi Zhi, and if you call it, it will retreat, provides a method to control the mountain spirit.
  • 再一名曰超空 - Another name for it is Chao Kong, introduces an alternative name.
  • 亦可兼呼之 - It can also be called, suggests that both names can be used.
  • 山精或如鼓赤色一足 - Mountain spirits sometimes look like drums, red in color, and have one foot, describes another type of mountain spirit.
  • 其名曰浑 - Its name is Hun, provides the name of this mountain spirit.
  • 或如人长九尺衣裘戴笠 - Sometimes they look like humans, nine feet tall, wearing fur coats and hats, describes another type of mountain spirit.
  • 名曰金累 - Its name is Jin Lei, provides the name of this mountain spirit.
  • 或如龙有五赤色角 - Sometimes they look like dragons with five red horns, describes another type of mountain spirit.
  • 名曰飞龙 - Its name is Fei Long, provides the name of this mountain spirit.
  • 见之皆可呼其名不敢为害 - When seen, they can be called by name and will not cause harm, provides a method to control these mountain spirits.
  • 晉永嘉之亂 - During the chaos of the Jin Dynasty's Yongjia period, refers to a specific historical period.
  • 宜陽有女子姓彭名娥 - In Yiyang, there was a woman named Peng E, introduces the main character.
  • 父母昆弟為長沙賊所掳 - Her parents and siblings were captured by the bandits of Changsha, describes the situation.
  • 負器出汲於溪 - Carrying a container, she went to the stream to fetch water, describes her action.
  • 與賊相格賊縛娥驅去溪邊將殺之際 - She fought against the bandits, who tied her up and took her to the stream to kill her, describes the conflict.
  • 因奔走向山山立開數丈平路如砥 - She ran towards the mountain, and the mountain suddenly opened, revealing a flat path, describes the miraculous event.
  • 群賊亦逐娥入山山遂崩合泯然如初 - The bandits followed her into the mountain, which then collapsed, burying them, describes the outcome.
  • 賊皆壓死山里頭出山外 - The bandits were all crushed to death, their heads sticking out of the mountain, describes the final result.
  • 娥所舍汲器化為石形似雞 - The container she left behind turned into a stone shaped like a chicken, describes a transformation.
  • 土人因號曰石雞山溪有娥潭 - The locals named the mountain 'Stone Chicken Mountain' and the stream 'Peng E's Pool', describes the naming of the place.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吳天門張蓋冬月 - Refers to a specific person or official during the winter in Wu Tianmen.
  • 孫皓 - Refers to Sun Hao, the last emperor of the Eastern Wu state during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 臨海 - Refers to the region of Linhai, a place in ancient China.
  • 山海經 - The *Shanhaijing* (Classic of Mountains and Seas) is an ancient Chinese text that describes mythical creatures and places.
  • 抱朴子 - *Baopu Zi* is a text by Ge Hong, a famous alchemist and writer of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 晉永嘉之亂 - Refers to the chaos during the Yongjia period of the Jin Dynasty, a time of political turmoil and migration.
  • 宜陽 - Refers to the region of Yiyang, a place in ancient China.
  • 長沙賊 - Refers to bandits from Changsha, a region in ancient China.
  • 娥潭 - Refers to a pool named after Peng E, the woman in the story.

[4654]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 新豐山 - Refers to a mountain in the new Feng County.
  • 唐髙宗朝 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Gaozong of Tang (650-683).
  • 神池 - A mystical or sacred pool.
  • 黄龙现 - The appearance of a yellow dragon.
  • 吐寳珠 - The dragon spits out a treasure pearl.
  • 改新豐縣為慶山县 - The renaming of the new Feng County to Qingshan County.
  • 慶山 - Refers to the mountain named Qingshan.
  • 昭應慶山 - Refers to the Qingshan mountain in the area of Zhaoying.
  • 亦不知從何飛來 - It is unknown from where it came.
  • 夜過聞有聲如雷 - At night, it was heard passing with a sound like thunder.
  • 疾若奔驰 - Moving as fast as a galloping horse.
  • 土石乱下 - Soil and stones falling chaotically.
  • 直坠新丰西南一村 - Directly falling into a village southwest of new Feng.
  • 因山为坟 - Using the mountain as a burial mound.
  • 起持国寺 - Establishing the Chiguo Temple.
  • 瓮峰 - Refers to the mountain named Wangdong.
  • 华岳云台观 - Refers to the Yuntai Temple on Mount Hua.
  • 方之上有山崛起 - A mountain rises above the flat ground.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 綠珠井 - Well associated with the story of Liu Ling and the beauty named Green Pearl (绿珠).

  • 汲飲此水者誕女必多美麗 - Suggests that those who drink the water from this well will give birth to many beautiful daughters.

  • 異哉 - An exclamation expressing surprise or wonder.

  • 葛稚川云 - Refers to Ge Zhi, a Taoist alchemist.
  • 余祖鴻臚少時嘗為臨沅令 - States that Ge Zhi's ancestor served as the magistrate of Lin Yuan.
  • 此縣有名家世夀考或出百歲或八九十後徙去子孫轉多夭折 - Describes a family in the county that lived long lives, but after moving away, their descendants had shorter lives.
  • 他人居其故宅後累世壽考 - Indicates that others who lived in the old house also had long-lived descendants.
  • 由此乃覺是宅所為而不知其何故 - Suggests that the reason for the long lives was attributed to the house, but the cause was unknown.
  • 疑其井水硃赤乃試掘井左右得古人埋丹砂数十斛去井數尺此丹砂汁因泉漸入井是以飲其水而得夀況乃餌鏈丹砂而服之乎 - Describes a suspicion that the well water was red due to ancient buried cinnabar, and a test that revealed the presence of cinnabar, leading to the conclusion that drinking the water could extend life.
  • 火井 - A well associated with fire.
  • 縱廣五尺深二三丈 - Describes the size of the well.
  • 在蜀都者時以竹板木投之以取火 - Indicates that in Shu (Sichuan), people used to throw bamboo or wood into the well to get fire.
  • 後人以家燭火投井中即滅息至今不復然也 - States that later, people tried to use their own candles to light the well, but the fire went out, and the well no longer produced fire.
  • 鹽井 - A well associated with salt production.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 葛稚川 - Ge Zhi, a famous Taoist alchemist and writer from the Eastern Jin Dynasty.

  • This long sentence describes the well in Sichuan, the method of using it to get fire, and the observation by Zhuge Liang.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 陵州盐井 - Refers to a salt well in Lingzhou, a specific location.
  • 后汉仙者沛国张道陵之所开凿 - Describes that the salt well was excavated by Zhang Daoling, a legendary figure from Pei State during the Eastern Han period.
  • 周回四丈深四十尺 - Describes the dimensions of the well, with '周回' meaning circumference and '深' meaning depth.
  • 置灶煮盐一分入官二分入百姓家 - A specific arrangement where one part of the salt produced goes to the government and two parts go to the people.
  • 因利所以聚人因人所以成邑 - A compound phrase indicating that the salt well attracts people due to its economic benefits, and these people form a community.
  • 万岁通天二年右补阙郭文简奏卖水一日一夜得四十五万贯 - A historical event where Guo Wenjian, a Right Censor, reported that selling water from the well generated a significant amount of money.
  • 百姓贪其利人用失业 - Indicates that people became unemployed due to the lucrative nature of the well.
  • 井上又有玉女庙 - Refers to a temple dedicated to a jade maiden built above the well.
  • 古老传云比十二玉女尝与张道陵指地开井遂奉以为神 - An ancient legend about twelve jade maidens who helped Zhang Daoling excavate the well, leading to their veneration as deities.
  • 又俗称井底有灵不得以火投及秽汚 - A popular belief that the well has a spirit and should not be polluted with fire or filth.
  • 曾有汲水误以火坠即吼沸涌烟气冲上溅泥漂石甚为可畏 - Describes a dangerous incident where someone accidentally dropped a fire into the well, causing it to erupt violently.
  • 或云泉脉通东海时有败船木浮出 - A folk belief that the well's spring connects to the East Sea, occasionally bringing up debris from shipwrecks.
  • 御井 - Refers to a well used by the imperial court.
  • 善和访旧御井故老云非可饮之井地卑水柔宜用濯 - Describes an ancient well that is not suitable for drinking but is good for washing.
  • 开元中以骆驼数十防入大内以给六宫 - During the Kaiyuan era, dozens of camels were used to transport water from this well to the imperial harem.
  • 唐贞元十四年春三月寿州随军王迪家井忽然沸溢十日 - In the 14th year of the Zhenyuan era, the well in Wang Di's home in Shouzhou suddenly boiled and overflowed for ten days.
  • 汲见井底有声如婴儿之声 - The sound heard at the bottom of the well was like that of a baby.
  • 至四月兄弟二人盲又一人死家事狼狈之应验 - By April, two brothers became blind, one died, and the family faced severe misfortune.
  • 贾耽在滑台城北命凿八角井以镇黄河 - Jia Dan ordered the construction of an octagonal well in the north of Huatuo City to stabilize the Yellow River.
  • 使人于凿所侦之有一老父来观问曰谁人凿此井也吏曰相公也父曰大好手但近东近西近南近北也 - A man was sent to investigate the well site, and an old man asked who had ordered the well to be dug. The official replied that it was Jia Dan, and the old man commented that it was well done but should be closer to the east, west, south, or north.
  • 吾是井大夫也 - The old man identified himself as the 'well god.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 后汉 - Refers to the Eastern Han period (25-220 AD).
  • 张道陵 - Zhang Daoling, a Taoist figure and founder of the Way of the Celestial Masters.

  • 贞元十四年 - The 14th year of the Zhenyuan era (808 AD) during the reign of Emperor Dezong of Tang.

  • 贾耽 - Jia Dan, a Tang dynasty official and cartographer.
  • 滑台城 - Huatuo City, a historical location in Henan Province.
  • 黄河 - The Yellow River, one of the major rivers in China.
  • 井大夫 - A term for a deity or spirit associated with wells.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 陵州盐井后汉仙者沛国张道陵之所开凿 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous and can be read as 'the salt well in Lingzhou, excavated by the Eastern Han immortal Zhang Daoling from Pei State.'

  • 百姓贪其利人用失业 - This phrase can be read as 'people became unemployed due to the lucrative nature of the well.'
  • 井上又有玉女庙 - This phrase can be read as 'there is also a temple dedicated to a jade maiden on top of the well.'
  • 古老传云比十二玉女尝与张道陵指地开井遂奉以为神 - This phrase can be read as 'an ancient legend says that twelve jade maidens once helped Zhang Daoling point out where to dig the well, and they are now venerated as deities.'
  • 又俗称井底有灵不得以火投及秽汚 - This phrase can be read as 'it is also said that the well has a spirit and should not be polluted with fire or filth.'
  • 曾有汲水误以火坠即吼沸涌烟气冲上溅泥漂石甚为可畏 - This phrase can be read as 'once someone accidentally dropped a fire into the well, causing it to erupt violently, with smoke rising and mud and stones being thrown up.'
  • 或云泉脉通东海时有败船木浮出 - This phrase can be read as 'some say that the spring of the well connects to the East Sea, occasionally bringing up debris from shipwrecks.'
  • 御井 - This term can be read as 'imperial well.'
  • 善和访旧御井故老云非可饮之井地卑水柔宜用濯 - This phrase can be read as 'the old well in Shouzhou, which is not suitable for drinking but is good for washing, was visited by the imperial court.'
  • 开元中以骆驼数十防入大内以给六宫 - This phrase can be read as 'during the Kaiyuan era, dozens of camels were used to transport water from this well to the imperial harem.'

  • 汲见井底有声如婴儿之声 - This phrase can be read as 'the sound heard at the bottom of the well was like that of a baby.'
  • 至四月兄弟二人盲又一人死家事狼狈之应验 - This phrase can be read as 'by April, two brothers became blind, one died, and the family faced severe misfortune.'
  • 贾耽在滑台城北命凿八角井以镇黄河 - This phrase can be read as 'Jia Dan ordered the construction of an octagonal well in the north of Huatuo City to stabilize the Yellow River.'

  • 吾是井大夫也 - The old man identified himself as the 'well god.'

After the burial, Chéng Bì had different intentions and went back to the mountain to see the Daoist, seeking more elixir. The Daoist did not give it to him. Chéng Bì then took a sword and threatened him. When he could not get the elixir, he cut off the Daoist's hands. When that did not work, he cut off his feet. The Daoist's face did not change. Chéng Bì became even angrier and cut off his head. When he took off his clothes, he found a red bag behind his elbow. When he opened it, it contained the elixir. Chéng Bì was delighted and took the elixir down the mountain. Suddenly, he heard someone calling his name. He turned around and saw that it was the Daoist. Chéng Bì was greatly surprised and said to himself, 'I did not expect to come to this. I cannot receive the elixir; the gods will surely punish you. You will end up like me.' Then he disappeared. Chéng Bì obtained much elixir and turned much red copper into gold, which was slightly redder and better than ordinary gold.

  • 辅国惊愕失据而冁然者不已悲号者更涕泣交下 - Vivid description meaning 'Fu Guo was shocked and lost his composure; the one that laughed continued laughing while the one that wailed began to cry,' indicating a dramatic and inexplicable event.
  • 辅国恶其怪遂碎之为粉没于厠中 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Fu Guo, disgusted by the strange occurrence, smashed them into powder and buried them in the latrine,' indicating his reaction to the event.

  • 始碎辟邪辅国嬖奴慕容宫知异常物 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'when the talismans were first broken, Fu Guo's favorite eunuch, Murong Gong, recognized them as unusual objects,' suggesting the eunuch's awareness of their significance.

  • 当时议者以竒香异宝非人臣之所蓄也 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'at the time, people argued that such rare fragrances and treasures were not meant to be possessed by a minister,' indicating a moral or political judgment.

  • 其色碧而干似苦竹叶细于杉 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'its color resembled green jade, and its dry leaves were like those of a bitter bamboo, finer than those of a cedar,' providing a detailed description of the plant.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 焉乃以囊中五宝示恒 - 焉乃 means 'thus' or 'then,' introducing the action of showing the five treasures.
  • 其一曰黄天符 - 其一曰 means 'the first one is called.' 黄天符 is a talisman with black and yellow colors and a heavenly symbol.
  • 形如笏长可八寸余濶三寸 - 形如笏 means 'shaped like a tablet.' 长可八寸余濶三寸 describes the dimensions: about eight inches long and three inches wide.
  • 上圆下方近圆有孔 - 上圆下方 means 'round on top and square on the bottom.' 近圆有孔 means 'nearly round with a hole.'
  • 黄玉也色比蒸栗潭若凝脂 - 黄玉也 means 'it is yellow jade.' 色比蒸栗潭若凝脂 describes the color: like steamed chestnuts, clear as if it were congealed fat.
  • 辟人间兵疫邪疠 - 辟 means 'ward off' or 'protect against.' 人间兵疫邪疠 refers to 'human warfare, epidemics, and evil diseases.'
  • 其二曰玉鸡毛文悉备 - 其二曰 means 'the second one is called.' 玉鸡毛文悉备 describes the texture: the feathers of a jade chicken are fully present.
  • 白玉也王者以孝理天下则见 - 白玉也 means 'it is white jade.' 王者以孝理天下则见 means 'if the ruler governs the world with filial piety, it will appear.'
  • 其三曰谷璧白玉也径五六寸 - 其三曰 means 'the third one is called.' 谷璧白玉也径五六寸 describes the size: a white jade grain璧, about five or six inches in diameter.
  • 其文粟粒自生无异雕镌之状 - 其文粟粒自生 means 'its pattern is like grains of millet, naturally formed.' 无异雕镌之状 means 'without the appearance of being carved.'
  • 王者得之则五谷丰稔 - 王者得之则五谷丰稔 means 'if the ruler obtains it, the five grains will be abundant.'
  • 其四曰王母玉环二枚亦白玉也径六寸好倍于肉 - 其四曰 means 'the fourth one is called.' 王母玉环二枚 refers to 'two jade rings of the Queen Mother.' 亦白玉也径六寸 means 'they are also white jade, six inches in diameter.' 好倍于肉 means 'the hole is twice the size of the thickness.'
  • 王者得之能令外国归向 - 王者得之能令外国归向 means 'if the ruler obtains it, he can make foreign countries submit.'
  • 其玉色光彩溢发特异于常 - 其玉色光彩溢发 means 'its jade color shines brightly.' 特异于常 means 'is particularly unusual.'
  • 卢恒曰玉信玉矣安知宝乎 - 卢恒曰 means 'Lu Heng said.' 玉信玉矣 means 'it is indeed jade.' 安知宝乎 means 'how can one know if it is a treasure?'
  • 其光皆射日仰望不知光之所极也 - 其光皆射日 means 'their light all shot towards the sun.' 仰望不知光之所极也 means 'looking up, one cannot see where the light ends.'
  • 恒与县吏同视咸异之 - 恒与县吏同视 means 'Heng and the county officials looked together.' 咸异之 means 'all found it strange.'
  • 翼日侁至恒白于侁曰 - 翼日 means 'the next day.' 侁至恒白于侁曰 means 'Shen arrived, and Heng reported to Shen, saying.'
  • 宝葢天授非人事也 - 宝葢天授 means 'the treasure is a gift from heaven.' 非人事也 means 'not something human.'
  • 侁覆验无异叹骇久之 - 侁覆验 means 'Shen re-examined.' 无异 means 'found no difference.' 叹骇久之 means 'was amazed and shocked for a long time.'
  • 即具事白报节度使崔圆圆异之 - 即具事白报 means 'immediately reported the matter.' 节度使崔圆 means 'Jiedushi Cui Yuan.' 圆异之 means 'found it strange.'
  • 征真 - 征真 means 'to summon Zhenru.'

[4757]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 甚多文本曰 - A formal way of introducing a person or text, meaning 'Many texts say' or 'As many texts state.'
  • 吾人冠帔何制度之异 - A question about the difference in the system or rules regarding the attire of the wearer.
  • 夫道在于方圎之中 - A philosophical statement about the nature of the path or principle being discussed, emphasizing the balance between square (方) and circle (圎).
  • 仆外服圆而心方正 - A description of the external appearance and inner character, suggesting a round outer form but a square, upright heart.
  • 相时之仪也 - Means 'in accordance with the times' or 'appropriate to the current era,' indicating that the attire is suitable for the present time.
  • 衣服皆轻细 - Describes the garments as light and fine.
  • 何土所出 - A question about the origin of the material.
  • 此是上清五铢服 - Indicates that the garments are made of 'Shangqing Wu Zhu' fabric, a type of fine silk.
  • 比闻六铢者天人衣 - A comparison to 'Liu Zhu' clothing, which is associated with heavenly beings.
  • 五铢之异 - Refers to the difference between 'Wu Zhu' and 'Liu Zhu.'
  • 尤细者则五铢也 - States that the finer of the two is 'Wu Zhu.'
  • 谈论不觉日晚 - Means 'the conversation continued until evening,' indicating that the discussion was engaging and prolonged.
  • 乃别去 - Means 'then they parted ways.'
  • 才出门而忽不见 - Describes the sudden disappearance of the person.
  • 文本知是异人 - Indicates that the person realizes the visitor is an extraordinary individual.
  • 每下朝即令伺之 - Means 'every time he comes down from court, he orders someone to wait for him.'
  • 到则话论移时 - Means 'when he arrives, they talk for a while.'
  • 后令人潜送诣其所止 - Means 'later, he secretly sent someone to follow him to where he stayed.'
  • 出山亭门东行数步 - Describes the direction and distance the person travels after leaving.
  • 于院墙下瞥然而没 - Means 'he suddenly disappeared at the wall of the courtyard.'
  • 文本命工力掘之 - Means 'Wenben ordered workers to dig.'
  • 三尺至一古墓 - Describes the depth and discovery of an ancient tomb.
  • 墓中无余物惟得古钱一枚 - States that the only thing found in the tomb was an ancient coin.
  • 外圆心方钱之状也 - Describes the shape of the coin as round on the outside and square on the inside.
  • 青衣铜衣也 - Refers to the greenish color of the copper.
  • 五铢服亦钱之文也 - States that 'Wu Zhu' clothing is also inscribed with the text of the coin.
  • 文本虽知之而钱帛日盛 - Means 'Wenben knew this, but the value of money and silk increased daily.'
  • 至中书令十年忽失古钱所在 - States that after ten years as a mid-level official, the ancient coin was suddenly lost.
  • 文本遂薨 - Means 'Wenben then died.'
  • 出博异志 - Indicates the source of the story, 'Bo Yi Zhi' (Records of Strange Things).
  • 元和初洛阳村百姓王清佣力得钱五锭 - Describes the beginning of the story, where Wang Qing, a villager from Luoyang, earned five ingots of money.
  • 因买田畔一枯栗树将为薪以求利 - Means 'he bought a dry chestnut tree beside a field to use as firewood for profit.'
  • 惊惧失斤而走 - Means 'the neighbor was frightened, dropped the axe, and ran away.'
  • 及明王清率子孙薪之 - Means 'the next morning, Wang Qing and his descendants cut the tree for firewood.'
  • 复掘其根下得大瓮二散钱实之 - Describes finding two large jars filled with scattered coins at the roots of the tree.
  • 王清因是获利如归 - Means 'Wang Qing then gained wealth as if returning home.'
  • 十余年巨富 - Indicates that Wang Qing became very wealthy over the next ten years.
  • 遂甃钱成龙形号王清本 - Means 'he then arranged the coins into a dragon shape and called it 'Wang Qing's Essence.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 熇然焦灼 - Means 'burning and scorching,' emphasizing the intense heat.
  • 求亲信察问之 - Means 'to seek trusted people to investigate,' indicating a request for verification.
  • 金盆贮水 - Means 'a golden basin filled with water,' describing the setup.
  • 浸白龙皮 - Means 'to soak white dragon skin,' indicating a specific ritual or practice.
  • 置于坐末 - Means 'placed at the end of the seat,' describing the position of the dragon skin.
  • 龙皮有新罗僧得海中 - Means 'the dragon skin was obtained by a Silla monk from the sea,' providing historical context.
  • 海旁居者能得 - Means 'those who live by the sea can obtain it,' indicating the availability of the dragon skin.
  • 自鱼扈有老人见而识之 - Means 'an old man from Fish Guard saw and recognized it,' adding a personal anecdote.
  • 僧知李好竒因以金帛赎之 - Means 'the monk knew Li Haoqi and redeemed it with gold and silk,' explaining the acquisition.
  • 暖金带辟尘簪 - Means 'warm gold belt and dust-repelling hairpin,' describing valuable items.
  • 希世之寳 - Means 'rare treasures of the world,' emphasizing the value of the items.
  • 及李南迁悉于思溪沈溺 - Means 'when Li moved south, he submerged all of them in the Si Creek,' describing the disposal of the items.
  • 使昆仑没取之 - Means 'he sent a Kunlun diver to retrieve them,' indicating the effort to recover the items.
  • 云在鳄鱼穴中竟不可得矣 - Means 'it was said they were in the crocodile cave, but they could not be found,' describing the unsuccessful search.
  • 东都平泉庄 - Means 'the Pingquan Villa in the Eastern Capital,' identifying the location.
  • 有虚槛前引泉水潆回疏凿 - Means 'there are open railings in front leading to winding water channels and carefully excavated pools,' describing the landscape.
  • 江山景物之状 - Means 'the appearance of mountains and rivers,' emphasizing the natural beauty.
  • 有巨鱼脇骨一条长二丈五尺 - Means 'there is a giant fish rib bone one and a half zhang long,' providing a specific measurement.
  • 其上刻云防昌二年海州送到 - Means 'on it is inscribed that it was sent from Haizhou in the second year of Fanchang,' providing historical context.
  • 庄东南隅即征士韦楚老拾遗别墅 - Means 'in the southeast corner of the villa is the former residence of the recluse Wei Chulao, who was a pick-up man,' identifying the location.
  • 楚老风韵髙邈雅好山水 - Means 'Wei Chulao had a high and distant style and a love for mountains and waters,' describing his character.
  • 李居廊庙日以白衣累擢谏署 - Means 'Li lived in the government and was repeatedly promoted from a civilian position to a censorial office,' explaining his career.
  • 故数年之间无所不有时文人有题平泉诗者 - Means 'so within a few years, there was nothing he did not have, and sometimes poets wrote poems about Pingquan,' describing the accumulation of wealth and fame.
  • 陇右诸侯供语鸟日南太守送花钱威势之使人也 - Means 'the princes of Longyou provided parrots, and the governor of Rizhuan sent money, both as agents of power,' describing the gifts and their significance.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 西国蕃人言与胡榛子同 - This phrase could be interpreted as the Western people saying that '阿月' is similar to '胡榛子'.
  • 一年榛子二年阿月 - This comparison suggests that '榛子' takes one year to mature while '阿月' takes two years.
  • 耆旧説周秦时河南雨酸枣遂生野酸 - This sentence is a bit ambiguous but can be understood as the elderly saying that during the Zhou and Qin periods, rain in Henan turned into sour jujubes, which then grew into wild sour jujubes.
  • 酸枣之甚小者为野酸 - This sentence can be interpreted as the very small sour jujubes being considered wild sour jujubes.
  • 俗言蒲萄蔓好引于西南 - This phrase can be interpreted as a common saying that grapevines grow well in the southwest direction.
  • 我在邺遂大得蒲萄 - This sentence can be interpreted as the speaker obtaining many grapes when they were in Ye.
  • 有软 - This description can be interpreted as a type of soft grape.
  • 君殊不体物何得不言似生荔枝 - This sentence can be interpreted as suggesting that the grape does not resemble a living lychee.
  • 末夏渉秋尚有余暑 - This phrase can be interpreted as indicating that late summer into early autumn still has lingering heat.
  • 酒醉宿酲 - This phrase can be interpreted as referring to the state of being drunk and having a hangover.
  • 掩露而食 - This phrase can be interpreted as describing eating grapes under the moonlight.
  • 甘而不饴 - This phrase can be interpreted as indicating that the grapes are sweet but not overly sweet.
  • 酸而不酢 - This phrase can be interpreted as indicating that the grapes are slightly acidic but not overly sour.
  • 道之固以流沫称竒 - This phrase can be interpreted as suggesting that the grapes are so good that they are described as a marvel.
  • 况亲食之者 - This phrase can be interpreted as emphasizing that those who eat them personally would find them even more wonderful.
  • 此物出自大宛 - This phrase can be interpreted as indicating that the grapes come from Da Yuan (a region in Central Asia).
  • 张骞所致 - This phrase can be interpreted as indicating that Zhang Qian brought the grapes back.

  • 胡椒出摩伽陁国呼为昧履支 - 胡椒出自摩伽陁国（今印度），称为昧履支。
  • 其苗蔓生茎极柔弱叶长寸半有细条与叶齐条上结子两两相对 - 胡椒的植物特征：蔓生植物，茎非常柔软，叶子长度约为一寸半，有细条与叶子平行，果实成对生长在细条上。
  • 其叶晨开暮合合则裹其子于叶中子形似汉椒至芳辣 - 胡椒的叶子早晨张开，傍晚闭合，闭合时将果实包裹在叶子中，果实形状像中国的花椒，非常芳香和辛辣。
  • 阿勃参出佛林国长一丈余皮色青白叶细两两相对花似蔓菁正黄子似胡椒赤色斫其枝汁如油 - 阿勃参出自佛林国，长度超过一丈，皮色青白，叶子细小，成对生长，花像蔓菁，颜色正黄，果实像胡椒，颜色赤红，砍断枝条会有油状汁液。
  • 以涂癣疥无不瘳其油极贵价重千金 - 用阿勃参的油涂抹癣疥，无一不愈，其油非常珍贵，价值千金。
  • 麻黄茎端开花花小而黄簇生子如覆盆可食至冬枯死如草及春却青 - 麻黄在茎端开花，花小而黄，果实簇生，形状像覆盆，可以食用。麻黄在冬天枯死如草，到春天又变绿。
  • 唐河东裴同父患腹痛数年不可忍嘱其子曰吾死后必出吾病子从之出得一物大如鹿条脯悬之久干有客窃之其坚如骨削之文彩焕发遂以为刀欛子佩之在路放马抽刀子割三棱草坐其上欛尽消成水客怪之回以问同同泣具言之后病状同者服三棱草汁多验 - 唐代河东的裴同父患有腹痛多年，无法忍受，嘱咐儿子在他死后一定要找出他的病根。儿子遵照嘱咐，找到了一个像鹿条脯大小的东西，悬挂后变干。有客人偷了它，发现它像骨头一样坚硬，削开后纹理光彩焕发，于是用它做刀柄。儿子佩带它，在路上放马，抽出刀子割三棱草，坐在上面，刀柄完全融化成水。客人感到奇怪，回去问裴同，裴同哭泣着详细说明，后来病状相同的人服用三棱草汁大多有效。

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Long sequences such as '趙瞿者病癞歴年眾治之不愈垂死' require careful parsing to convey the sequence of events.
  • Complex sentences like '或云不如及活流棄之否則后子孙转相注易其家乃为赍粮而送之置山穴中' need clear separation of clauses to maintain logical flow.
  • Sequences describing interactions, such as '仙人行过穴口而哀之具问讯焉瞿知其异人乃叩头自陈乞哀于仙人以嚢药赐之教其服法', require careful translation to preserve the sequence of actions and emotional context.
  • Long sequences describing effects and instructions, such as '瞿服之百许日疮愈顔色丰悦肌肤玉泽仙人又过视之瞿谢受更生活之恩乞丐其方仙人告之云此是松脂耳此山中更多此物汝链之服可以长生不死', require clear separation of clauses to maintain narrative flow.
  • Sequences describing the spread of practices and varying outcomes, such as '其间闻瞿服松脂如此于是竞服其多力者乃车运驴誓积之盈室服之逺者不过一月未觉有大益辄止有志者难得如是也', require careful translation to preserve logical progression.
